source: Tracks and profiles are the real published routes — pulled from the organisers' Ride with GPS routes (2025–2026) and komoot tours/collections (2019–2024). 2020 was cancelled (COVID-19).

editions.title: The tough inaugural-era route
editions.desc: One of the earliest editions and a seriously hard one, made tougher by difficult weather. Four long checkpoint-to-checkpoint stages from Bishkek around the central Tien Shan.
Cancelled due to the COVID-19 pandemic — no race was held.

editions.title: Talas to Issyk-Köl
editions.desc: The third edition, back after the pandemic — roughly 1,804 km and 30,500 m of climbing from Talas in the north-west to the shore of Issyk-Köl near Balykchy.

editions.title: A brutal, high edition
editions.desc: 1,924 km with a savage 36,600 m of climbing. Highest point 4,150 m, lowest 730 m — from the dusty hills of Jalal-Abad to the river crossings of the Burkhan valley and the shores of Song-Köl.
highlights: Dirt road through the hills of Jalal-Abad (≈511 km in)
Burkhan Valley — remote river crossings and yurts (≈1,137 km)

editions.title: Eight stages, Karakol to the north shore
editions.desc: A grand loop of the central and eastern Tien Shan in eight checkpoint stages — over the Chong-Ashuu, out to remote Kel-Suu near the Chinese border, across Song-Köl and home over the Kegety pass.

editions.title: Fifteen checkpoint stages
editions.desc: The 2024 edition, run over fifteen checkpoint stages across the Tien Shan.

editions.title: Osh to Karakol
editions.desc: A southern start near Osh finishing in Karakol — 1,956 km with 27,861 m of climbing, the official track published on Ride with GPS.

editions.title: The longest ever — Talas to Cholpon-Ata
editions.desc: The eighth edition and the longest route yet, with the most climbing by a solid margin and only ~20% paved. It starts in Talas in the north-west, features a new 150 km wilderness link from Baetov toward Torugart and a hike over the Ukok pass past Kök-Ulok lake, then a classic finish over the Kegety and Kök-Ayryk passes to Cholpon-Ata.
